title: QA Orbit-Dirac Bracket: second-class constraints on S_9 physical subspace {b=1, b^2-be-e^2=1}; X invertible, [b,e]_orbit=0, strong-zero verified
source_attribution: Blaschke, D.N., Gieres, F., 'On the canonical formulation of gauge field theories and Poincare transformations,' Nucl. Phys. B 965 (2021), arXiv:2004.14406, eqs (5.32)-(5.39) — Dirac-bracket construction from second-class constraints, canonical source for the {F,G}_D formula. Mannheim, P.D., 'Equivalence of light-front quantization and instant-time quantization,' Phys. Rev. D 102 025020 (2020), arXiv:1909.03548 — slice/path (observer/invariant) framing that motivates the QA-side firewall reading. Cross-map docs/theory/QA_QFT_ETCR_CROSSMAP.md §4.2 (MC-3, MC-4). Prior-art audit docs/theory/QA_QFT_COMMUTATORS_PRIOR_ART.md §5b — C3 tuple wedge recast as symplectic lift base bracket. Preliminary T-invariant docs/theory/empirical/etcr_t_invariant_check.py: I(b,e) = (b^2-be-e^2)^2 mod m, Cassini-squared. Paper section papers/in-progress/qft-etcr-orbit-quotient/section.md §4.4. Substrate: [191] Tiered Reachability. Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-21.

dynamics.note: Fibonacci-like QA step. Under T_F the unsquared Cassini b^2-be-e^2 flips sign per step, so the squared scalar I = (b^2-be-e^2)^2 is T-invariant.

base_bracket.name: symplectic_lift_of_tuple_wedge
base_bracket.note: Symplectic lift of the tuple wedge {(b1,e1),(b2,e2)} = b1*e2 - b2*e1 (prior-art C3 recast). Equivalent to the standard 2D Poisson bracket with {b, e} = 1, extended to polynomial functions in Z[b, e] via formal partial derivatives.

physical_subspace.description: Subset of H_raw = {1..9}^2 defined by simultaneous vanishing of phi_1 and phi_2 mod 9. Selects two points (1,8), (1,9) on the +1 branch of the Cassini-squared level I=1 (Cosmos orbit).

constraint_polynomials.name: phi_1
constraint_polynomials.label: cassini_level_plus1_branch
constraint_polynomials.role: first-class-like level-set constraint (T-flips to -phi_1 mod sign squared)

x_matrix_symbolic.note: Recomputed by validator symbolically from phi_1, phi_2 via [phi_a, phi_b] := (d_b phi_a)(d_e phi_b) - (d_e phi_a)(d_b phi_b).

strong_zero_declarations.description: Validator independently recomputes [phi_a, F]_orbit for a in {1,2} and F in {b, e} at every witness; all must equal 0 mod 9. Parallels Blaschke-Gieres eq (5.38) — constraint functions become strong invariants under the Dirac bracket.

bracket_b_e_instantiation.description: MC-4 deliverable 5: [F, G]_orbit instantiated for F=b and G=e on the physical subspace. Value must equal 0 mod m at every witness — reflects that b and e are not independent in the Dirac-bracket quotient (constraints reduce the effective degrees of freedom).

scope_boundaries.covered: m=9, T_F dynamics, two-constraint family, +1 Cassini branch of I=1 Cosmos orbit
not_covered: m=24 — requires mixed I-level + period-n constraint family (see CROSSMAP §4.2 last paragraph)
Other QA step operators beyond T_F — T-invariance of I depends on the Fibonacci-like step
-1 branch of I=1 level set — symmetric construction; not included in v1
Satellite and Singularity orbits (both at I=0) — would require auxiliary fixed-point constraint
Unequal-k CCR invariant (cert-C) — follow-up cert, depends on cert-D stability
